theAwakening.title: Interlude III: The Awakening
theAwakening.body: A violent tremor knocks you prone. As you clamber to your feet, the colossal form of Cthulhu rises from the heart of the drowned city, its terrible will pressing against your mind. Struggling against the ancient one, you and the survivors of the expedition flee toward the surface, the city collapsing into the churning sea behind you.

returnToArkham.title: Interlude IV: Return to Arkham
returnToArkham.body: Your sleep on the voyage home is fraught with nightmares of the sleeping god and the drowned city. You wake each morning more certain that what you witnessed was no dream—and that the doom you glimpsed beneath the waves is following you back to Arkham.

anOfferYouCantRefuse.title: Interlude I: An Offer You Can’t Refuse
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude1: _Interlude 1_: “What you have recovered is no mere curio. It was bought at great expense from a dealer in Egypt, who inherited it from a relative who bought it in Peking, you know how it goes,” Randall smirks as you admire the ancient artifact. It is a vaguely humanoid figure, sitting in a stooped position, with a heavy, octopoid head and squid-like features. There is something malevolent in its expression, something unwholesome in the very way it was sculpted. You find it difficult to look away.
anOfferYouCantRefuse.agencyDetectiveOrCriminal: _If your investigator has the Agency, Detective, or Criminal trait:_ Randall’s operatic tale of the artifact’s acquisition seems needlessly contrived. You have every reason to believe the ancient statue was obtained through less than legitimate means. Sadly, this tells you nothing of the relic’s true origins.
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude1Continued:
“This is a singular relic of prehistoric, dare I say, pre-human origins,” Randall’s voice breaks the spell. The rest of the eccentric antiquarian’s story boggles the mind. He describes a record of some ancient cosmic struggle, and how he believes that all of earth is on the cusp of an apocalypse, much like the one that destroyed the statue’s creator.

“In all of human history—in every separate mythology—there exists a story of a great flood. A cataclysm. There are legends of a great city-state: a bastion of knowledge and might, that sank beneath the waves. Humankind has named it many things. Atlantis. Mu. Lemuria. But its true name, a name that predates history, is _R’lyeh_.

“Through much painstaking research, and the assistance of a mad sailor named Gustaf, I have pinpointed the relative coordinates of the ancient city. A stretch of ocean in the South Pacific where many sailing vessels have shipwrecked or disappeared with no explanation. Which brings me to my offer. I need someone bold and resourceful to lead an expedition to the ancient city. Someone to bring back evidence of its ancient knowledge, study its secrets, record its wonders. I need that person to be you,” Randall says, looking to you, then to Ruby and Andy.

“Should you accept, I shall absolve you of any remaining debt and provide you enough money to retire comfortably. Very comfortably. And, of course, lend my assistance for your other concerns.”

Your mind wanders back to those other “concerns.”
anOfferYouCantRefuse.chooseTask: In player order, each investigator must choose a different Task and read its text.

walkInFaith.title: Task: Walk in Faith
walkInFaith.body: All your life, you have believed in something greater than yourself, something that guides your hand and directs your path. Months ago, you recognized the unblinking eye set in the stained glass of Tillinghast’s shop from your dreams. It was a sign. You can’t help but feel that this is the reason you were led here. You have no choice but to say yes.

toeTheLine.title: Task: Toe the Line
toeTheLine.body: You were a bright star, rising through the ranks—until your sudden and ignominious fall from grace. Your former colleague saw to it that you’d never come by honest work, so you turned to Tillinghast to make a living. You’ve always hoped he might help you get your old position back and restore your good name.

noPlaceLikeHome.title: Task: No Place Like Home
noPlaceLikeHome.body: Mr. Tillinghast has always been good to you. When you were down on your luck, it was Tillinghast who got you off the streets, who paid for a warm bed and a hot meal at Ma’s Boarding House. The money he’s promised for this job will finally get you back on your feet. You know he’s good for it.

goodMoney.title: Task: Good Money
goodMoney.body: Over the years, you’ve had to do plenty of things you’d never admit in polite society. But that’s all over now. This is your chance for the fresh start Tillinghast promised you years ago when he paid off your old debt. With what Tillinghast is promising, you should finally be able to live life on your own terms—so long as you survive one more cockamamie errand.

doNoHarm.title: Task: Do No Harm
doNoHarm.body: People have always depended on you when things go wrong. Randall has seen your talents put to good use over the course of your work with him; the slippery businessman has never given you easy work, but you’ve always risen to the challenge. If you nail this last job, he’s promised you enough to live your dreams.

proveYourWorth.title: Task: Prove Your Worth
proveYourWorth.body: You’ve heard them talking about you behind your back. “Dead weight,” they called you. You’ll show them. Randall has promised you everything you need to rise to the top, to show those catty bastards what you’re really made of. You need this job to prove to everyone that you’re not the failure your peers say you are.

dreamsOfDestruction.title: Task: Dreams of Destruction
dreamsOfDestruction.body: Every night for the past year, you have been wracked with awful nightmares. Visions of a terrible calamity. Streets in ruin. Spectral forms of the drowned. And above it all some nameless, looming form. You entered Tillinghast’s shop hoping to find answers in his occult tomes, and soon found yourself ensnared in his growing enterprise. Randall has promised you answers.

plumbTheDepths.title: Task: Plumb the Depths
plumbTheDepths.body: You never meant to get involved with Randall, or the mob, or any of this. But you can never suppress the inexorable itch to understand the world around you. Although you have been warned time and again that your curiosity would be the end of you, you can’t deny the allure of the unknown. This job will make your career, if you can just play your cards right.

anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude2:
_Interlude 2_: You narrow your eyes at Randall. There’s something he’s not telling you. Some catch. There’s always a catch with Tillinghast. Just as there’s always one more job to be done. You ask him what it is and he sighs.

“I thought it was obvious, but this expedition is likely to be rather perilous. Which is why I thought to entrust it to you. You’ve proven yourself to be quite resourceful. And as I said, I intend to reward you handsomely.”

The investigators must decide (choose one):
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude3:
_Interlude 3_: There has to be a catch. Tillinghast wouldn’t offer such tempting bait otherwise. You’re through with him. You shake your head no.

“Then get out,” Tillinghast says icily, pointing to the door. “I have no further use for you. Rest assured, however, that if you speak a word of this to anyone, you will regret it.” Without a word, you walk out of the shop, leaving behind whatever wonders—and dangers—Tillinghast had in store for you.

  • The investigators lose the campaign.
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude4:
_Interlude 4_: Whatever his reasons for burying the lede, the offer is too tempting to refuse. Ruby and Andy nod along with you in agreement. Tillinghast’s face breaks into a familiar smirk. “How lovely. I’d hoped you would say yes. Truly, this is the opportunity of a lifetime. I assure you this will change everything.”

After Andy negotiates for a comprehensive supply of film, and Ruby ups her commission, Tillinghast outlines the plan for the expedition. Tomorrow, a train will take you to San Francisco, where the Cassandra will sail you down to the provided coordinates in the South Pacific. There is an excited, almost childlike glee as the antiquarian outlines the details. “I’ve sent supplies enough to last the entire expedition several months if needed. That said, be prepared for anything.”

Proceed to _Interlude II: Expedition to R’lyeh_

recommended.noPlaceLikeHome: Recommended for Survivor ({'{'}survivor{'}'}), Drifter, Hunter, or Wayfarer investigators.
recommended.walkInFaith: Recommended for Guardian ({'{'}guardian{'}'}), Mystic ({'{'}mystic{'}'}), Believer, Blessed, Chosen, Hunter, or Sorcerer investigators.
recommended.toeTheLine: Recommended for Guardian ({'{'}guardian{'}'}), Agency, Detective, Hunter, Police, or Veteran investigators.
recommended.goodMoney: Recommended for Rogue ({'{'}rogue{'}'}), Civic, Criminal, Entrepreneur, or Socialite investigators.
recommended.proveYourWorth: Recommended for Agency, Performer, Silver Twilight, or Socialite investigators.
recommended.doNoHarm: Recommended for Assistant, Civic, Medic, Veteran, or Warden investigators.
recommended.dreamsOfDestruction: Recommended for Mystic ({'{'}mystic{'}'}), Artist, Clairvoyant, Cultist, Cursed, Dreamer, Performer, or Sorcerer investigators.
recommended.plumbTheDepths: Recommended for Seeker ({'{'}seeker{'}'}), Agency, Clairvoyant, Detective, Miskatonic, Scholar, or Reporter investigators.

expeditionToRlyeh.title: Interlude II: Expedition to R’lyeh
expeditionToRlyeh.body:
It is early evening when you first see the ancient city. You emerge from your cabin to see Andy and Ruby staring out at a jagged, bleak skyline off the starboard bow. The ancient city is a tangle of collapsed towers, labyrinthine boroughs, and jagged paths that loop in on themselves like a rat’s nest. Andy nearly drops his camera as a choppy wave lifts and then drops the nose of the ship with a sharp crash.

“You’re seeing this. It’s real. Tell me it’s real,” Andy says, furiously loading more film into his scuffed camera. You and the rest of the crew marvel at the mind-bending skyline with a mixture of awe and dread. Seawater pours from the mouths of alien effigies and ancient sepulchres. Strange ephemeral shapes flit in and out of the obsidian canyons of R’lyeh. It is unlike anything built by human hands.

You catch yourself saying the city’s name under your breath. Even the unflappable Ruby seems dumbstruck as the ship sails past a ring of rocky shoals into a broad cove. After making landfall, the expedition camps on the black-sanded beach, at the foot of a towering obsidian spire. Menacing, gargantuan doors set in the tower’s base loom like silent sentinels. There is hardly a word between the expedition members on the first night; Andy spends the evening poring over an obsidian stone set with glowing glyphs, while Ruby lovingly reorganizes her tool bag.

In the morning, you discuss which route to take. Tillinghast has provided enough diving equipment, climbing gear, and rations to explore inland for at least two weeks before you would need to return to the beach. Andy and Ruby are at odds, however. Andy wants to follow the coastline to the ruined western wall near the mouth of the cove. Ruby, perennially comfortable with heights, wants to take to the skyline to map the city. She points excitedly at a distant, cathedral-like structure further inland.

Depending on which character you want to follow, you will either explore the ancient city from west to east, or east to west. You must decide, as a group:

label.west: Follow Andy along the shoreline to the Western Wall. Proceed to _Western Expedition._
label.east: Accompany Ruby through the Obsidian Canyons toward a massive inland structure. Skip to _Eastern Expedition._

expeditionToRlyeh.westernExpedition:
_Western Expedition:_ Something about the distant, ruined wall and submerged spires calls to you. After agreeing to accompany Andy westward, he sighs with relief. “I can handle myself, of course, but if we have to go diving, I’d…” he looks blankly into the distance for a moment. “I don’t want to worry you, but I could swear I saw something human-shaped swimming below. I’ll be glad for the company.” You look past the black water to the edge of the cove, suddenly apprehensive. Something crests the waves for a moment, then disappears. You have the feeling of being watched.

“Two weeks, then,” says Ruby, hefting a coil of rope over one shoulder. “We’ll meet back here in two weeks. Don’t forget the diving suits. And don’t get yourself killed. I would hate to lose my favorite rival.”
expeditionToRlyeh.easternExpedition: _Eastern Expedition:_ Since you landed, you haven’t been able to take your eyes off the phantasmagoric skyline. After agreeing to accompany her eastward, you catch the faintest hint of a smile from Ruby. “Fine, you can come along. As long as you don’t hold me back,” she smirks. A fell wind whistles through the perilous canyons, smelling of ancient decay and another, sweeter smell. Storm clouds churn in the distance threateningly.
expeditionToRlyeh.theExpeditionHeadedWest: In your Campaign Log, record *the expedition headed west*.
expeditionToRlyeh.andyVanNortwick: Choose an investigator to add the Andy Van Nortwick story asset to their deck. It does not count toward their deck size. This card can be found in the Expedition encounter set, indicated by the following icon:
expeditionToRlyeh.westernChaosTokens: Remove 1 {'{'}tablet{'}'} token from the chaos bag. Add 1 {'{'}cultist{'}'} token to the chaos bag for the remainder of the campaign.
expeditionToRlyeh.theExpeditionHeadedEast: In your Campaign Log, record *the expedition headed east*.
expeditionToRlyeh.rubyStandish: Choose an investigator to add the Ruby Standish story asset to their deck. It does not count toward their deck size. This card can be found in the Expedition encounter set, indicated by the following icon:
